steps.title: Understand what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The y-intercept is the value of $y$ where the graph crosses the y-axis. This occurs at $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph at x = 0
steps.content: Looking at the graph where the parabola intersects the y-axis (the vertical line at $x = 0$), the curve passes through the point $(0, -3)$. Therefore, $y = -3$.
